items.title: ACT Greens choose Jo Clay as new leader in membership vote

children.content: Jo Clay is elected leader of the ACT Greens in a party vote, with Andrew Braddock as deputy leader, ushering in a new era after Shane Rattenbury's 14 years at the helm.

gallery.altText: A woman with dark hair and a green blouse speaks, standing outside near a green hedge.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with dark hair and a green blouse speaks, standing outside near a green hedge.

items.title: Three new bulk-billing GP clinics opening in Canberra

children.content: Three new bulk-billing GP clinics are opening in Canberra, but questions remain about how they will be funded and staffed long-term.

image.altText: Doctor tools hanging on the wall of a medical consultation room

items.title: Man who stabbed wife in front of daughter found guilty of attempted murder

children.content: An ACT Supreme Court jury has found a Canberra man guilty of attempted murder after he stabbed his wife during a violent attack in the family's Gungahlin home.

image.altText: The ACT Supreme Court towards dusk.

items.title: Teen charged twice in three days over alleged stolen car pursuits

children.content: A 14-year-old boy charged twice in three days after allegedly leading police on stolen car pursuits is among several incidents prompting the police union to call for tougher bail laws in the ACT.

gallery.altText: A screenshot of police dash-cam footage showing officers making an arrest beside the road.
Man wearing glasses standing outside a police station.

thumbnail.altText: A screenshot of police dash-cam footage showing officers making an arrest beside the road.
Man wearing glasses standing outside a police station.

items.title: Museum's macabre merchandise, outback evidence from the Chamberlain trial

children.content: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

items.title: Hikers lost near Kosciuszko found by artificial intelligence drone

children.content: Rescuers were able to locate the hikers less than five hours after they were reported missing.

image.altText: A grey image with a red dot.

items.title: Does Pauline Hanson's claim of growing support in the ACT pass the pub test?

children.content: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

gallery.altText: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson answers a question following her address at the National Press Club

thumbnail.altText: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson answers a question following her address at the National Press Club

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia

children.content: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after being slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

gallery.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

thumbnail.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

items.title: Social media giants face fines for failing to hand over documents

children.content: Labor wants to double the fines for tech giants who refuse to hand over documents that could prove they are failing to enforce Australia’s social media age ban.

image.altText: A woman in an burnt orange suit, gestures with her hands while standing at a lectern.

items.title: ABC presenter Trevor Chappell to hang up mic after 26 years

children.content: After decades of keeping night owls and shift workers company on ABC radio across Australia, Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell decides to call it a day at the age of 65.

image.altText: Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell wears a blue shirt and stands near the arts centre

images.altText: Julio Enciso runs off in celebration of a goal
Kai Havertz runs off and grabs his jersey in celebration
Brazil players celebrate wildly in a group after the winning goal against Japan

articles.title: How a punt on a trial in Italy created the Socceroos' next star

children.content: Cristian Volpato was rejected by two Australian soccer academies. His family dropped everything to go for a trial in Italy. That was the making of Australia's next star.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato #20 of Australia strikes the ball during the FIFA World Cup

children.content: Paraguay takes an unlikely lead over Germany, while Brazil's Gabriel Martinelli scores in the 96th minute to break Japanese hearts. Follow live.

items.title: Germany and Paraguay set for grandstand finish, Brazil breaks Japan hearts
Ill-fated midnight mountain walk in freezing conditions led to 12-day search

children.content: The NSW coroner praises emergency services for "persistence" during the 12-day search for a hiker who set off on an ill-fated midnight mountain walk in sub-zero temperatures while wearing sandals.

image.altText: A woman with blond hair smiling at the camera.

items.title: 'Loving' mum remembered after Airbnb guest found guilty of killing her

children.content: Maree Vermont's sons say they never thought hosting Airbnb guests would be dangerous for their mother, who was proud of the home she had built.

image.altText: Two men stand in front of a stone house with signs of fire damage.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz strikes likely to disrupt shipping again

children.content: Fresh attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz have exposed the fragility of the 60-day ceasefire deal between the US and Iran, which was meant to allow ships to transit through the waterway again.

image.altText: A drone view of vessles anchored in the Strait of Hormuz as seen from Musandam, Oman

items.title: Giveaway club RS Rewards accused of 'ongoing and misleading conduct'

children.content: One of Australia's biggest "rewards clubs" has been banned from running any gaming activity in New South Wales for 18 months after allegedly breaking the law.

image.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: NDIS shell company boom sparks crackdown

children.content: The federal government says it is moving to crack down on the growing trade in "off-the-shelf" NDIS providers.

gallery.altText: A middle aged white woman with short fair hair and red suit sitting in an office chair

thumbnail.altText: A middle aged white woman with short fair hair and red suit sitting in an office chair

items.title: Don't lodge too early and other tax return tips from experts

children.content: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

image.altText: woman looking at laptop on the couch with paperwork

items.title: Quiz: Will you raise the white flag on these tricky flag questions?

children.content: How many stars are there on the US flag? And how many stars are on the Europe flag?

image.altText: The Australian, Aboriginal and Torres Strait flags fly in Canberra.

items.title: BalletTok, blackface and nudes: Inside the world of professional ballet

children.content: Ariane Beeston, the author of an acclaimed memoir detailing her experience with postpartum psychosis, has penned her first work of fiction, Don't Forget to Smile.

gallery.altText: Woman with long dark hair sitting wearing white and looking at camera

thumbnail.altText: Woman with long dark hair sitting wearing white and looking at camera

items.title: Bondi terror attack survivor received 'influx of hate' hours after shooting

children.content: Deepfake and altered images depicting a Bondi Beach terror attack survivor holding an Academy Award while covered in blood spread "like wildfire" online just hours after the shooting, a royal commission has heard.

gallery.altText: Arsen Ostrovsky in a suit and blue tie outside a building

thumbnail.altText: Arsen Ostrovsky in a suit and blue tie outside a building

items.title: Homemade production of 'lethal weapons' on the rise

children.content: Since Queensland strengthened its firearm laws in March, one person a fortnight has been charged with possessing a blueprint to make 3D-printed guns.

image.altText: an open shoulder bag with several glocks inside

items.title: Court rejects Trump's final bid to overturn E Jean Carroll verdict

children.content: The US Supreme Court declined to hear US President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5 million USD verdict in a case in which a jury found him liable for sexually abusing and defaming E Jean Carroll.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and orange tinted glasses

items.title: PM-in-waiting Andy Burnham lays out plan to fix 'broken' British politics

children.content: Andy Burnham, the British MP tipped to replace the outgoing prime minister Sir Keir Starmer, promised "radical change" to the nation's "broken" political system as he laid out his vision to lead the country on Monday.

gallery.altText: A man with a suit jacket on speaking at a lecturn and gesturing with his right hand.

thumbnail.altText: A man with a suit jacket on speaking at a lecturn and gesturing with his right hand.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: 'It's who we are': Albanese issues impassioned defence of multiculturalism

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese issues a defence of CGT changes from the budget and Australia's multicultural society.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese seated in a TV studio wearing a suit.

items.title: The medicine safety system most Australians have never heard of

children.content: A little black triangle on medicine information leaflets is meant to make it easier to report adverse effects of new medications. Problem is, many Australians don't know what it means.

gallery.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

thumbnail.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

items.title: 'Save costs': Raft of new measures taking effect in NSW from tomorrow

children.content: Motorists, public transport users and wage earners are the biggest winners under new changes across NSW starting from July 1.

image.altText: A wallet with an NAB and Qantas card and a $5 and $10 notes poking out

items.title: Six killed in German 'family tragedy' shooting, say police

children.content: Two people have been arrested after a shooting in the northern German town of Stade.

image.altText: Crime scene techs stand next to an emergency services vehicle on cobbled street.

items.title: Venezuela's earthquake death toll rises amid magnitude-4.6 aftershock

children.content: As a magnitude-4.6 aftershock struck northern Venezuela, the nation's acting president announced "transitional camps" would be created to house the homeless.

image.altText: an aerial image of apartment buildings reduced to rubble

items.title: Putin says Russia will press on despite fuel shortages

children.content: President Vladimir Putin has said Russia will press ahead with its battlefield aim of fully capturing four Ukrainian regions, rejecting diplomatic overtures from Ukraine.

image.altText: Explosion at Moscow oil refinery after Ukrainian drone attacks.

items.title: Penelope Keith, star of 'The Good Life' and 'To the Manor Born', dead at 86

children.content: Beloved British actress Dame Penelope Keith, who rose to fame in the hit 1970s UK sitcom "The Good Life", has died aged 86.

items.title: Sam Kerr returns to the United States after Chelsea exit

children.content: Matildas striker Sam Kerr has returned to the United States following her successful stint at London giants, Chelsea.

image.altText: Sam Kerr high fives Chelsea teammates Ellie Carpenter and Keira Walsh.

items.title: Australian Kasatkina finally wins on grass to kick-off Wimbledon run

children.content: After a winless grass-court swing, Australian Daria Kasatkina becomes Australia's first winner at Wimbledon for 2026.

image.altText: Daria Kasatkina hits a forehand at Wimbledon.

items.title: Simpson Desert has never looked better but where are the tourists?

children.content: Birdsville, a pilgrimage point for outback travellers, navigates the ripple effects of flood, fuel fears and the cancellation of a massive music festival, when the surrounding country has rarely looked so green and alive.

image.altText: A sandbar overlooking flooded desert landscape.

gallery.altText: A sandbar overlooking flooded desert landscape.

thumbnail.altText: A sandbar overlooking flooded desert landscape.

items.title: Worker says forestry transition program 'decimated' Gippsland's small towns

children.content: The Victorian Forestry Transition Program is ending. In the regions that relied on the timber trade, the question is whether it worked.

image.altText: Image of a small general store, with a petrol bowser out the front on the footpath.
